body.title: Cache _migrations table lookups per-process (9,264 hits, avg 121ms)
body.result: 75254143 — module-level _MIGRATION_CACHE in cortex.py + 3 callsite refactors (scope_backfill gate, meaning_to_me gate, _run_schema_migrations). 6 tests pass. Eliminates SELECT 1 FROM _migrations per Cortex init.
body.description:
SELECT 1 FROM _migrations WHERE name = ? fires 9,264 times per session (avg 121ms, worst 1,152ms) — every call to the migration checker re-queries the DB even for already-completed migrations. Cache the set of completed migration names in a module-level or class-level frozenset loaded once at process start; skip the DB lookup for any name already in the set.

**Affected files:** lab/utility_closet/db_proxy.py (migration check logic, lines ~183 and ~744). Update db_proxy.py top-of-file docstring to note the cache.
**Design rules:** no-sqlite: reads _migrations table (Postgres), in-process cache only (no new persistence). oop-first: PGDatabaseProxy is already a class — cache goes as an instance or class attribute. docs-in-code: update db_proxy.py docstring. test-plan-or-why-not: applies.
**Scope boundary:** IN — cache the per-name lookup. OUT — cache invalidation across processes, restructuring _migrations table, multi-process cache coherence (single-process only).
**Test plan:** Unit test — mock _migrations table with 3 entries, call migration check 100x, assert DB query called exactly 3 times total (one per name). Integration test — boot Igor, confirm _migrations SELECT count in db_queries.log drops from ~9k to <20.
